Welcome to Campus

President's Welcome Letter

Dear Family,

Congratulations.

We are proud to inform you that you have been officially accepted to Hankerson University: The Real HU. Your acceptance is not based on grades, test scores, or legacy status. It is based on something older and stronger: bloodlines and chosen bonds, shared history, and a love that has carried us through generations, through seasons and through change.

In 1978, the Hankerson family made a decision to gather on purpose. What began as an idea became an annual reunion tradition. What became a tradition grew into a family institution. This year, on July 17 - 19, we return as students and teachers of that legacy.

Hankerson University exists to do three things:

  • 1Honor those who came before us
  • 2Invest in those who are here now
  • 3Prepare those who are coming next

Your presence matters. Your stories matter. Your laughter counts as participation.

Below, you will find your HU Syllabus, which outlines the weekend's schedule, expectations, and details. Please review it carefully so you are fully prepared for this term at The Real HU.

With love,
The Sister-Cousin Connection

This Year's Dedication

This year's reunion is held in honor of Gwen Porter, who founded and hosted the very first Hankerson gathering in 1978.

Reunion Theme

Hankerson University: The Real HU. A family that gathers on purpose, every summer, to honor, invest, and prepare.

Student Life

Style Guide

Southern Collegiate Brunch · Family Banquet attire. We invite guests to dress within the following palette. Soft earth tones, jewel accents, and warm neutrals are encouraged.

Women

Sundresses or midi dresses · coordinated sets · dress pants with blouse · linen jumpsuits · heels, flats, or elevated sandals.

Please, no denim.

Men

Slacks · blazers or lightweight suits · button-down shirts or knit polos · loafers or dress shoes.

To preserve the elegance of the occasion, please avoid denim and athletic sneakers.

Fabrics & Feel

Light, breathable, and polished textures are encouraged: linen, lightweight cotton, satin or silk, soft knits, and subtle structured fabrics.

Our Legacy

Family Timeline

In 1978, the Hankerson family made a decision to gather on purpose, and a tradition was born in Tampa, Florida. Nearly five decades later, it has grown into a family institution nearly fifty summers strong.

Family Trivia
?
Press the button for a reunion memory
Reunion Founders

Hankerson University, The Real HU, traces its very first gathering to 1978 in Tampa, Florida, founded and hosted by Gwen Porter. What she began as a single afternoon of family fellowship has now carried the Hankerson name across 48 summers and dozens of cities.

This year's reunion is hosted by the Sister-Cousin Connection: Shantae Bolden, Tara Deloach, Anita Gibson, Keishon Graham, Sharmaine Lewis, Lashauna Bolden Morris, and Latoyor Owens.

Hall of Legacy · To the Oldest Male and Female

I thought upon the elderly and whispered a prayer, giving thanks to God for their sojourn here. Such kind and gentle souls from generations ago, what they are to us, they will never know.

A sure and beaten path; a guide along the way. The dedicated laborer who's now old and gray. Patriarchs and matriarchs, and early pioneers. The bridge that has brought us as we've journeyed here.

Soldiers of great sacrifice, treasured more than art. We honor, love and cherish them deep within our heart.

~ Walterrean Salley (Savannah, GA)
May God continue to bless our seniors, the roots living among us.

Celebrate the Family

To the Youngest

Nothing brings more joy to a family than the birth of a child: the first smile, the first tears, the first baby laugh. May God bless the newest branches of our family tree.

To the Newlywed

"When you realize you want to spend the rest of your life with somebody, you want the rest of your life to start as soon as possible." May God bless the newlyweds.

To the Graduates

"Think of your degree not as a ticket to the good life, but as your ticket to change the world." Congratulations, onward and upward!

Historiography · The Hankerson Coat of Arms

Illustrated by a heraldic artist, the Hankerson Coat of Arms employs symbols upon a shield following the traditional system of heraldry that originated in medieval times. Heraldry developed among the nobility in the 11th century, instituted to distinguish one man in armor from another amid the confusion of battle.

In the traditional language of heraldry, the Hankerson Coat of Arms is described as divided horizontally: the upper half white with a red diagonal band, the lower half red with three white roses, symbolic of England.

In 1972, fewer than 375 households in the United States carried the Hankerson name. This history does not represent any single family's individual lineage, and no genealogical representation is intended or implied.

Family Anthem · Lift Every Voice and Sing

Lift every voice and sing,

Till earth and heaven ring,

Ring with the harmonies of Liberty;

Let our rejoicing rise

High as the listening skies,

Let it resound loud as the rolling sea.

Sing a song full of the faith that the dark past has taught us,

Sing a song full of the hope that the present has brought us;

Facing the rising sun of our new day begun,

Let us march on till victory is won.

by James Weldon Johnson (1900) · popularly known as "The Negro National Anthem," first performed by schoolchildren in Jacksonville, Florida.

Black Family Pledge

Because we have forgotten our ancestors, our children no longer give us honor. Because we have lost the path our ancestors cleared kneeling in perilous undergrowth, our children cannot find their way. Because the old walls of our ancestors have faded beyond our hearing, our children cannot hear us crying.

Therefore, we pledge to bind ourselves to one another: to embrace our lowliest, to keep company with our loneliest, to educate our illiterate, to feed our starving, to clothe our ragged. To do all things, knowing that we are more than keepers of our brothers and sisters. We are brothers and sisters.

~ Dr. Maya Angelou, May 14, 1986

Family Tree

The Family as it Began. The story, as told to me.

In Loving Memory

God's Plan

And although it seems sometimes to us our prayers have not been heard, God knows our every need without a single word. And He will never forsake us, even though the way seems steep, for always He is near to us, a tender watch and keep. And in good time He'll answer us, and in His love He'll send greater things than we have asked, and blessings without end.

In loving memory of

Add any names of loved ones who have passed since the last printing, to keep this list current for 2026.

"Those we love don't go away, they walk beside us every day."

Family Bingo

Tap a square as it happens this weekend. Get five in a row and shout "HU!" Your progress is saved on this device.

People I Met This Weekend

Bring a pen: collect names, faces, and one fun fact from every new (or newly reconnected) cousin.

Commencement

Alumni Pledge & Parting Words

As a graduate of this term, I pledge to:

  • Honor those who came before me
  • Invest in those who are here now
  • Prepare those who are coming next
  • Carry the Hankerson legacy forward, wherever I go

"Like branches on a tree, we all grow in different directions, yet our roots remain as one."

Well, family, another reunion has come and gone. The Sister-Cousin Connection thanks you so very much for joining us for the Hankerson's 48th reunion. Planning this was not an easy task, but a great deal of love and forethought went into it. We hope you enjoyed yourselves as much as we did planning it!

As we come to the conclusion of another memory-filled weekend, we wish you safe travels. Our love goes with you, along with our prayers for blessings of peace, good health, and prosperity.

Until next time,
The Sister-Cousin Connection
